conclusion: No HARD pigeonhole wall (only 246/577348 mixed-label cells). Neither estimator bounds the ceiling from above. The evidence that ~0.9663 is the practical feature ceiling is CONVERGENT/EMPIRICAL: 13 diverse strong models span only 0.00075 and the residual error is structural - 84% of GALAXY->STAR errors sit at redshift<0.15 (median 0.073) where redshift, the dominant feature, is ~0 for both stars and low-z galaxies and loses discriminating power. The physical tie-breaker SDSS uses (morphology: point-source vs extended) is ABSENT from these 10 features. ~28% of errors are >0.90-confident-wrong (label-noise/feature-aliasing) which also caps MEASURED accuracy. Reaching 0.97 (+0.0037, ~5x the experiment spread) requires NEW INFORMATION (morphology / extra bands), not more model search.
knn_validity_note: Neighbour-based Bayes estimate is INVALID as a ceiling here: even with sky-coords dropped and redshift up-weighted, k-NN tops out at ~0.921 bal-acc (weights r=1/3/6 -> 0.9069/0.9190/0.9212), BELOW the GBM models 0.966. k-NN under-performs because the decision boundary is not locally smooth in raw feature space; categoricals and feature interactions carry signal neighbourhoods miss. So 0.90/0.92 are weak-classifier floors, not the Bayes ceiling.
